article_title: Pentagon and Linux Foundation Launch OCUDU, an Open-Source Software Stack Aimed at Becoming the Linux of 5G and 6G Networks

content_preview.title: Pentagon and Linux Foundation Launch OCUDU, an Open-Source Software Stack Aimed at Becoming the Linux of 5G and 6G Networks
content_preview.summary: The Linux Foundation launches the OCUDU Ecosystem Foundation with 47 members including AMD, Nokia, NVIDIA, and Verizon to build an open-source radio access network stack for 5G and 6G, backed by the Pentagon's FutureG office.
content_preview.body_excerpt: The Linux Foundation announced the formation of the Open Centralized Unit Distributed Unit Ecosystem Foundation at Mobile World Congress in Barcelona on March 1, 2026, creating a new public-private initiative to develop a [production-ready open-source software stack for the centralized and distributed unit layers of radio access networks](https://www.linuxfoundation.org/press/linux-foundation-announces-ocudu-ecosystem-foundation-to-accelerate-open-source-ai-ran-innovation). The project, which or...

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid telecom infrastructure article with clear context on Pentagon involvement and Open RAN landscape.
editor_notes.source_verification: Linux Foundation press release: verified — 47 members, Premier members list, FutureG office, MWC 2026 launch confirmed. NVIDIA newsroom: verified — AI-native platform commitment, telecom partnerships, AI-RAN Alliance confirmed. SiliconANGLE: 404 but claims corroborated by other two sources.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior OCUDU coverage.
